snippet.title: Leveraging Data to Transform Health with Sarah London, Centene Corporation
snippet.description:
Centene is a leading healthcare enterprise committed transforming the health of the community, one person at a time. The company offers high-quality, affordable health insurance coverage to nearly 1 in 15 individuals across the nation. Centene CEO Sarah London sat down down with Salesforce Chief Health Officer Fatima Paruk MD, MPH to discuss how Centene is tackling big issues like access to care and health equity, and how the company is leveraging technology and data to transform healthcare.

Watch the full session: https://community.hlth.com/home/videos/leveraging-data-to-transform-health

Want more content like this? Learn more about HLTH: https://www.hlth.com

snippet.description:
Welcome to King’s Guard Tours, a channel dedicated to royal traditions, ceremonial events and British pageantry 🇬🇧

We bring you immersive and engaging videos of the Changing of the Guard, Household Cavalry events at Horse Guards, and major Royal Family events 👑

Our videos often feature the King and Queen, the Prince and Princess of Wales (Prince William and Catherine), and other members of the Royal Family, including Prince Edward, Duchess Sophie, and Princess Anne.

Wherever you are in the world, you are very welcome here. If you enjoy royal traditions, history, and behind the scenes glimpses of these iconic events, make sure to subscribe to King’s Guard Tours 🇬🇧

🔔 Subscribe here: https://m.youtube.com/@KingsGuardTours

⭐ Consider becoming a channel member to support the channel: https://m.youtube.com/@KingsGuardTours/join

King’s Guard Tours is an independent channel and is not affiliated with the British Army, Household Division, Ministry of Defence, or the Royal Household.

snippet.title: ‘When I die, I don’t want Friends to be the first thing that’s mentioned:’ Matthew Perry #shorts
snippet.description:
Ottawa-raised actor Matthew Perry, known as Chandler Bing on Friends, was found dead in his hot tub over the weekend. He was 54. An investigation into how he died is ongoing, and it may take weeks before his cause of death is determined.

In a CBC Q interview from November 2022, Perry talked about his memoir, Friends, Lovers, and the Big Terrible Thing, which details his long journey with addiction, fame and recovery.  

He told Q host Tom Power why he didn’t want to be remembered only for Friends, but his work to help people with addiction. 

Read more: http://www.cbc.ca/news

»»» Subscribe to CBC News to watch more videos: http://bit.ly/1RreYWS

snippet.title: Maria Callas: one long breath
snippet.description:
Maria Callas: Take a deep breath and hold it. Carry that feeling of suspended tension all the way through to the end of the piece of music. 


Your music should have an underlying current energy that's always there, pushing the music forward. Drop that energy and the music dies. The audience collectively exhales.


Pavarotti, the famous tenor, used the jet engine analogy. A jet's engines are always buzzing in the background, carrying it forward. Turn those engines off mid-flight and you'll feel an eerie, *dead* silence.


Notice this with any great performer. They *grab* you from the first note and hoist you up, carrying you on a three-minute journey. Keeping you suspended requires a constant stream of energy. They don't *drop* you mid-song. 


---


Maria Callas soprano is one of classical music's most renowned musicians. Her voice was incredible, but she was perhaps best known for her sense of drama. She brought her characters to life. And had an infectious charisma. When she was on stage you simply couldn't look away. She's also one of the few classical musicians that was able to penetrate popular culture. A celebrity in her day, she was frequently seen at high-society functions, rubbing elbows with people like the Kennedys.

snippet.title: Is This Why Beyoncé Stopped Doing Interviews? (2001)
snippet.description:
On 27 February 2001, Beyoncé Knowles was interviewed on the Brit Awards red carpet along with her Destiny's Child bandmates Kelly Rowland and Michelle Williams. The three singers were thrown a curveball by ITN's Jon Gilbert, who took the opportunity to ask their thoughts about the foot and mouth epidemic that was devastating Britain's livestock at the time. It would be unfair to say that Beyoncé put her foot in her mouth. Given the circumstances, we think she did pretty well.

#Beyonce #DestinysChild #KellyRowland #MichelleWilliams #BritAwards

To search the ITN Archive collection on Getty Images, follow the link below:

https://www.gettyimages.co.uk/footage/itn

snippet.title: Grumpy king
snippet.description:
King Charles arrived to Westminster Abbey six minutes ahead of schedule and before his son.

The King was pictured looking grumpy as he arrived early at Westminster Abbey for the coronation ceremony.
